README.md.reason: Confirmed Factory owns governance aggregation while MCP remains an agent-facing transport surface.

CONTRIBUTING.md.reason: Required scoped evidence, honest current-state declarations, and no unsupported control claims.

schemas/repo-governance.schema.json.reason: Defined the allowed repository classification, runtime role, checks, exposure, and branch-protection fields.

blueprints/factory-run-signals/artifact-.reason: Kept credentials, live dashboard output, private source content, and raw tool payloads outside the governance declaration.

local_setup_divergence.notes: GitHub connector execution only. The descriptor is JSON-compatible YAML so standard-library repository CI can validate it without a private Factory checkout or third-party parser dependency.

roadmap_reconciliation.notes: MCP issue #724 closes the repository declaration gap supporting Factory #213 organization ruleset reconciliation. Live protection fields remain false until Factory #171 post-apply evidence exists.

state_refinement.release_state: No product lifecycle behavior changed. This change declares MCP governance posture and validates the declaration in CI.
invariants: MCP remains an agent-facing transport and does not become a product authority runtime.
Repository governance metadata must describe current live enforcement rather than desired future controls.
Public governance declarations must not expose credentials, private source content, raw tool payloads, live risk output, or security deltas.

rejected_or_collapsed_states: Rejected protected, enforced, and release-ready as product states.
Rejected setting branch-protection booleans true before live organization ruleset evidence exists.
minimization_review.rationale: A repository descriptor and deterministic validator are sufficient. No runtime state or authority change is needed.

end_of_session_capture.notes: Provider-neutral Factory worker implementation; CI and exact-head verification follow after PR creation.
